body.probe: Igor runs filter engram on a sample plan and emits PASS or FAIL with findings
body.title: Prove skill→engram: parse /filter into runnable engram nodes
body.result: skill_filter.py: 5 checks + run_filter() registered tool. SKILL_FILTER_ENTRY engram node seeded to DB (MCPCALL→run_filter, EMITIF→twm). PROC_INVOKE_FILTER habit trigger. node_executor probe confirmed: cursor traversal executes filter from DB node and returns FILTER RESULT PASS/FAIL. 30 tests, 1749 total green. Next: T-skill-to-engram-generalise (parser across all skills).
body.description: Parse ~/.claude/skills/filter/SKILL.md into engram nodes and seed to DB. Steps: (1) read skill markdown, (2) split on Step N / bash blocks / hard rules into cursor-sequenced nodes, (3) bash blocks → EMITIF with code_ref=run_bash, (4) hard rules → BRANCHIF guards, (5) skill references (/commit etc.) → FORKIF. Igor runs the resulting engram via existing execution stack. Probe: Igor executes /filter on a sample plan and produces a PASS/FAIL result using only his own engram — no Claude Code involved.
